revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:з”ҹеӨ§еҲҖ]]
'''Ikutachi''' (з”ҹеӨ§еҲҖ) is a sword appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. It is a {{ill|еӨ§еҲҖ|en|еӨ§еҲҖ|qid=Q1224104|lt=tachi|1=еӨ§еҲҖ}} that {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»зҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} brought back from {{ill|Ne no Kuni|ja|ж №д№Ӣе …е·һеӣҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135579218}} and is mentioned only in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}.
In modern contexts, it is often written as з”ҹеӨӘеҲҖ, but since tachi refers to curved swords from the Heian period onward, the term еӨ§еҲҖ is used for the straight swords of mythology and ancient artifacts, such as chokutЕҚ.
== Mythology == When {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§е·ұиІҙе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} fled to {{ill|Ne no Kuni|ja|ж №д№Ӣе …е·һеӣҪ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135579218}} to escape the pursuit of his brothers, the {{ill|Yaso-gami|ja|е…«еҚҒзҘһ|1=Yasogami|lt=Yasogami (80 gods)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaso-gami|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasogami|qq=}}, he encountered {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|й ҲеӢўзҗҶжҜҳеЈІе‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮ»гғӘгғ“гғЎ}}, the daughter of {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|й ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·е‘Ҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}}. The two deities fell in love at first sight.
However, Susanoo imposed various trials upon ЕҢkuninushi, some of which nearly cost him his life. In one instance, ЕҢkuninushi pretended to remove centipedes from Susanoo's hair, waiting until Susanoo fell asleep. Then, he tied SusanooвҖҷs hair to the pillars and attempted to flee with the sacred treasures, including the '''Ikutachi''', '''Ikuyumiya''' (з”ҹеј“зҹў), '''Amanokaguyumi''' (еӨ©и©”зҗҙ), and Suseribime herself. <ref>{{Cite book |language=ja |author=Tamio Tobe|title=ж—Ҙжң¬зҘһи©ұ - зҘһгҖ…гҒ®еЈ®йә—гҒӘгӮӢгғүгғ©гғһ |publisher=Shinkigensha |series=Truth In Fantasy 63 |date=October 2003 |isbn=978-4-7753-0203-3 |page=88 }}</ref>
As they fled, the Amanokaguyumi struck a tree and produced a loud sound, waking Susanoo, who pursued them to the {{ill|Yomotsu Hirasaka|en|Yomotsu Hirasaka|qid=Q22084535|lt=Yomotsu Hirasaka|1=Yomotsu Hirasaka}}, the boundary between Ne no Kuni and Ashihara no Nakatsukuni. There, Susanoo halted and proclaimed, "Use the tachi and bow to subdue the Yaso-gami who disobey you. Drive them into the mountains and rivers. You will become ЕҢkuninushi, the Utsushikunidama deity, and build a grand palace to live with Suseribime as your wife."
After receiving his new name, {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|ja|еӨ§еӣҪдё»зҘһ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§еӣҪдё»}} used the Ikutachi and Ikuyumiya to defeat the Yaso-gami and became the ruler of Ashihara no Nakatsukuni.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Migukurumitama Shrine|lt=Migukurumitama Shrine|lt=Mikukuru Shrine|ja|зҫҺе…·д№…з•ҷеҫЎйӯӮзҘһзӨҫ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikukuru Shrine|qq=}} - Believed to house the Ikutachi. * {{ill|ЕҢkuninushi|en|ЕҢkuninushi|qid=Q276944|lt=ЕҢkuninushi|1=ЕҢkuninushi}} (ЕҢkuninushi-no-ЕҢkami) * {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|й ҲдҪҗд№Ӣз”·|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮ№гӮөгғҺгӮӘ}} * {{ill|Nogi Wakaba Is a Hero|ja|д№ғжңЁиӢҘи‘үгҒҜеӢҮиҖ…гҒ§гҒӮгӮӢ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nogi Wakaba Is a Hero|1=Nogi Wakaba wa YЕ«sha de Aru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q20816907}} - The sword is featured as the weapon of the protagonist, Wakaba Nogi. * {{ill|Eight Clouds Rising|en|Eight Clouds Rising|qid=Q5348960|lt=Eight Clouds Rising|1=Eight Clouds Rising}} - Appears in the ancient arc as a divine sword given to ЕҢkuninushi by Mikachihiko. * {{ill|Hani in the Sky|ja|гҒҜгҒ«гҒ„гҒ„гӮ“гҒ–гҒҷгҒӢгҒ„|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hani in the Sky|1=Hani in the Sky|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11275696}} - One of the swords featured.
{{Japanese mythology}}
{{translated page|ja|з”ҹеӨ§еҲҖ|version=101099451|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:з”ҹеӨ§еҲҖ]]
